published_prior_art.cite: Leung2022ANI gives ANI IPW plus network HAC; the oracle collapse is a tautological cross-procedure benchmark once equality of projections/covariances is assumed.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure_v1 only studies finite partial-ID exposure-map bounds, not asymptotic ANI/HAC/NCR/randomization equivalence.

published_prior_art.cite: Leung2022ANI, Leung2023NCRI, BasseDingFellerToulis2024, GaoDing2025Network each cover one procedure family; none gives the proposed joint iff frontier.

WeinsteinNevo2026 derives bias from misspecified network interference and robust multi-network estimation; PuelzBasseFellerToulis2022 gives graph-theoretic randomization tests under general interference.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Puelz, Basse, Feller, and Toulis 2022 JRSSB on graph-theoretic randomization tests and Weinstein and Nevo 2026 Biometrics on misspecified network interference, both closer than the current randomization/misspecification discussion.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the separating frontier is the unobserved sensitivity parameter mu_perp, while flagship axis (b) requires a computable-from-observables sharp threshold.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: U_M,n is defined as a weighted omitted-tail operator while C_n(eta) and N_n(eta) are score-direction cones, so dist(U_M,n,N_n(eta)) needs an explicit vectorization/Hilbert-space identification.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement alternates between operator equivalence on the active cone and equality for the true omitted tail; mu_perp=0 only says the true tail lies in the common null cell, not that the covariance operators are identical on all active directions.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The display uses ordinary limits of random estimators, lim_n hat_tau_IPW,n and lim_n hat_tau_RT,n, without specifying plim, distributional convergence, or almost-sure convergence.

soundness_flags.label: Partial-interference randomization reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption partial-interference-corner gives graph separation, but it does not require Omega_n to be the within-group/design-compatible permutation group, so P_RT(Omega) need not equal the exposure-orbit projection.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the unobserved mu_perp frontier with an explicit observable optimization problem: given eta, output a closed-form or finite-dimensional program whose zero/nonzero value sharply separates equivalence and non-equivalence.

Add PuelzBasseFellerToulis2022 and WeinsteinNevo2026 to Related work and to the Conjecture 1/2 why-new blocks, explaining why biclique conditioning and misspecified-network bias do not subsume the proposed three-family frontier.
Define the common score space precisely so omitted-tail operators, cone elements, and covariance-operator differences all live in the same normed space.
Strengthen the partial-interference corollary with a design-orbit assumption tying Omega_n to the valid within-group permutation/orbit structure.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal to make the frontier observable, add the two missed comparators, and patch the score-space/convergence/orbit assumptions before Stage 0.
